wine_museums.name: Le Hameau Duboeuf
wine_museums.address: 796 Route de la Gare, 71570 Romaneche-Thorins, France
wine_museums.description: Europe's first dedicated wine park, holding over 3,000 objects related to winemaking housed across an immersive village-style complex. The Hameau Pass covers the Wine Village (museum, exhibitions, 3D and 4D cinema), the Winemaking Centre, botanical garden, and a structured Beaujolais tasting. A seasonal Jardin en Beaujolais garden opens April to September. The on-site boutique and restaurant are open daily from 8 am.
wine_museums.admission: Adults EUR 21; children 7-15 EUR 11; under 7 free; seasonal Hamlet-Garden Pass EUR 26 adults
wine_museums.opening_hours: Wednesday to Sunday 9:30 am to 7 pm; boutique daily from 8 am
wine_museums.tip: The Hameau-Garden combined pass (EUR 26) is the most complete option when the Jardin en Beaujolais is open, from April to September.

wine_museums.name: La Maison du Terroir Beaujolais
wine_museums.address: 24 Place de l'Hotel de Ville, 69430 Beaujeu, France
wine_museums.description: An interactive geology and viticulture museum in the historic capital of the Beaujolais, presenting the geological origins of the region's soils and how granite, schist, and clay create the diversity of the 12 appellations. Permanent exhibition covers vine cultivation cycles, the history of Gamay and Beaujolais wines, and includes a structured wine tasting of representative appellations. Open February to December, seven days a week.
wine_museums.admission: Adults EUR 7; children 6-16 EUR 3; under 6 free
wine_museums.opening_hours: February to December, daily 10 am to 12:30 pm and 2 pm to 6 pm; July and August closes at 7 pm

verified.address_quoted: 24 place de l'Hotel de ville, 69430 Beaujeu

wine_museums.name: Chateau de Corcelles Historic Cellar and Estate Tour
wine_museums.description: A 15th-century fortified chateau on an 87-hectare estate, designated a classified historic monument, with a cellar renowned for its impressively carved wooden casks and historic stonework. Guided visits pass through the chapel, medieval dungeons, the classified cellar, and the chateau kitchens, concluding with a tasting of the estate's organic Gamay and Chardonnay wines. Open year-round; audio-guided format available.
wine_museums.admission: Included with wine tasting; contact estate for group rates
wine_museums.opening_hours: April to October: Monday to Saturday 9:30 am to 12:30 pm and 1:30 pm to 6 pm; November to March: Monday to Saturday 10 am to 12:30 pm and 1:30 pm to 5:30 pm
wine_museums.tip: The cellar's 15th-century carved casks are among the most photographed wine heritage objects in the Beaujolais region; book ahead for group visits.

wine_museums.name: Chateau de la Chaize Historic Monument and Cellar
wine_museums.address: 500 Route de la Chaize, 69460 Odenas, France
wine_museums.description: A classified 17th-century chateau and wine estate covering 400 hectares, built between 1674 and 1676 and attributed to Jules Hardouin-Mansart with gardens by Andre Le Notre. The 108-metre-long cellar is itself a classified historic monument and one of the most remarkable in France. Guided tours cover the chateau exterior, the Le Notre garden, and the cellar, concluding with a tasting of the estate's organic Brouilly wines. Open seasonally; group and individual formats available.
wine_museums.admission: EUR 30 to 75 per person depending on format
wine_museums.opening_hours: Seasonal; open weekends and by appointment; contact estate for current schedule
wine_museums.tip: The 108-metre cellar is classified separately as a historic monument; the Saturday-Sunday visit with a charcuterie board is the most complete format available.

wine_museums.name: La Maison des Beaujolais Visitor Centre and Cave
wine_museums.description: The official regional visitor centre and largest Beaujolais appellation display space, with permanent exhibits covering all 12 appellations, the history of Gamay in Beaujolais, vineyard geology, and the Beaujolais Nouveau phenomenon. Rotating producer displays and seasonal guided tasting workshops led by Master Sommelier Guillaume Mithieux. Free entry to the exhibition space; tasting flights from EUR 15.
wine_museums.admission: Free entry to exhibition; tastings from EUR 15 per person
wine_museums.opening_hours: Open daily; check website for seasonal hours
